name: Urda and Hermitage of the True Cross Loop
name_original: Urda – circular por el pueblo hasta el molino del Tío Rufo
description: Enjoy an easy 2.8-mile (4.4 km) circular hike through Urda village, leading to the historic Windmill of "Tío Rufo" in about 1 hour and 11 mi
region_intro: Hiking around Urda offers varied terrain, characterized by rolling hills and historical landmarks. The region features routes that traverse landscapes with traditional windmills and castles, as well as paths through villages and agricultural areas. Elevations are generally moderate, making the area accessible for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Urda The most popular hiking route is Cerro Calderico – circular via Consuegra castle and windmills, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This easy route features historical structures and scenic views from Cerro Calderico. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra Calderina from Los Palancares, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This trail explores the Sierra Calderina region, offering a longer excursion into the natural landscape. Local hikers also love the Urda - circular through the village to Uncle Rufo's mill, a 2.8 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through the village and past a historic mill, often completed in about 1 hour 11 minutes. Hiking around Urda is defined by historical sites like castles and windmills, village paths, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy village strolls to moderate excursions into the surrounding countryside. The routes in Urda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 280 hikers have used komoot to explore Urda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Urda?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails around Urda, offering a variety of experiences. These include 11 easy routes, 16 moderate paths, and 1 challenging trail, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on hikes in Urda?
A: Hiking around Urda offers varied terrain, characterized by rolling hills and historical landmarks. You'll find routes that traverse landscapes with traditional windmills and castles, as well as paths through charming villages and agricultural areas. Elevations are generally moderate, providing accessible and scenic walks.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Urda?
A: Yes, Urda features several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019737626/urda-circular-through-the-village-to-uncle-rufo-s-mill" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urda - circular through the village to Uncle Rufo's mill&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.8-mile (4.4 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 11 minutes to complete, leading you through the village and past a historic mill.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while hiking near Urda?
A: The region is rich in historical sites. You can explore landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2654903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2661159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Windmills&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019744256/cerro-calderico-circular-via-consuegra-castle-and-windmills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Calderico – circular via Consuegra castle and windmills&lt;/a&gt; route is an excellent way to experience these historical structures and enjoy scenic views.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in the Urda area?
A: For a longer excursion, consider the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1020952719/sierra-calderina-from-los-palancares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Calderina from Los Palancares&lt;/a&gt;. This 8.0-mile (12.9 km) path explores the Sierra Calderina region, offering a more extensive journey into the natural landscape. For a difficult option, there's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209024156/from-fuente-el-fresno-mill-to-malagon-circular-from-fuente-de-fresno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Fuente el Fresno Mill to Malagón — circular from Fuente de Fresno&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 15 miles long.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Urda?
A: The routes in Urda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the historical sites, scenic views, and the accessibility of the trails for various abilities.

Q: Are there any natural viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Yes, many trails offer scenic viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calderico Hill&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent panoramic views of the surrounding area, including the famous windmills and castle. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019744256/cerro-calderico-circular-via-consuegra-castle-and-windmills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Calderico circular&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlight these scenic spots.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Urda?
A: Given the region's characteristics, spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking in Urda. The weather is typically milder, making it comfortable to explore the rolling hills and historical sites without the intense heat of summer or the cooler temperatures of winter.

Q: Are there any other attractions nearby that I can visit after a hike?
A: Absolutely! Besides the castles and windmills, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Valdehierro&lt;/a&gt; for a cultural experience or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/379059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Municipal Museum 🏛️ Consuegra&lt;/a&gt; to delve deeper into the local history and heritage.

Q: Are the trails around Urda suitable for families?
A: Yes, many trails around Urda are suitable for families, especially the easier routes. The generally moderate elevations and well-defined paths through villages and past historical sites make for enjoyable outings with children. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019737626/urda-circular-through-the-village-to-uncle-rufo-s-mill" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urda - circular through the village to Uncle Rufo's mill&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of a family-friendly option.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of hikes in Urda?
A: The hiking trails in Urda offer a range of difficulty levels. You'll find a good mix of easy and moderate routes, with 11 easy and 16 moderate options. There is also one difficult trail for those seeking a greater challenge. This variety ensures there's something for every hiker, from casual strolls to more demanding excursions.
tips: The first Hermitage of the Stmo. Cristo de la Vera Cruztiva hermitage in this town must have been built prior to the 16th century, then being located outside the walls of the town, under the dedication of Santo Cristo de la Caja, and it was, over the centuries, undergoing different reforms up to its current appearance. Of the original hermitage, practically only the rectangular shape of its main nave remains.

Manchego windmill rebuilt in 2006 by D. Eugenio González Tarjuelo. Currently owned by the González Viñas family. The only windmill of the eight that Urda had that is preserved for the use and enjoyment of all those who are passionate about these La Mancha mills and the milling culture.
The Plaza de la Constitución is beautiful, with rest areas and swings for the little ones.
Chapel dedicated to the Virgin in the dedication of the Immaculate Conception. It will be from the times of the Parish.
Famous and curious square in Urda, a good resting place.

Between La Mancha and the Montes de Toledo we find the town of Urda, 68 km from its capital. Its landscapes are dominated by heather and have been the land of beekeepers since ancient times, from which delicious honey is obtained. Its first settlers lived around the church of San Juan Bautista, built in the 13th century, it underwent major renovations in the 17th century thanks to the support of the Archbishopric of Toledo, which financed them.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Urda - circular through the village to Uncle Rufo's mill hike?
faqs.answer: This easy circular route typically takes around 1 hour and 12 minutes to complete, making it a perfect option for a leisurely stroll.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features easily accessible paths, making it suitable for all fitness levels. You'll walk through the village of Urda, experiencing local architecture and village life, leading to the historic windmill.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Windmill of "Tío Rufo", a reconstructed Manchegan windmill and the only preserved one of eight that once stood in Urda. Other notable spots include the Hermitage of the Most Holy Christ of the True Cross, Plaza de la Constitución, and the Chapel of the Conception, all within the charming village of Urda.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is an easy hike with a modest elevation gain and accessible paths, making it very suitable for families and hikers of all ages and fitness levels.

faqs.question: Is the Urda - circular through the village to Uncle Rufo's mill trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it passes through the village and potentially near livestock, so keeping your dog on a leash is recommended for their safety and the comfort of others.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Given its easy nature and passage through a village, this trail can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while cooler winter days can also be ideal. Summer might be warm, so an early morning or late afternoon hike is advisable during those months.

faqs.question: Where can I park for this hike?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, as the trail is circular and passes through the village of Urda, you can typically find parking within the village itself, especially near central points like the Plaza de la Constitución or other public areas.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access this trail. It is a publicly accessible route through the village and surrounding area.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will find it intersects with parts of the Camino Manchego de Santiago (19% of the route), Calle Santo (11%), and Calle Santa María del Monte (8%).

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restrooms along the way?
faqs.answer: As the trail goes through the village of Urda, you will find various amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and shops, particularly around the central plazas like Plaza de la Constitución. Restrooms may be available at these establishments.

faqs.question: What makes the Windmill of "Tío Rufo" special?
faqs.answer: The Windmill of "Tío Rufo" is significant because it is the only preserved Manchegan windmill out of eight that once existed in Urda. Reconstructed in 2006 and currently owned by the González Viñas family, it offers a unique glimpse into the region's traditional milling culture.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Most Holy Christ of the True Cross
